title: Caulanthus flavescens
taxonomy: Caulanthus flavescens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family includes a wide variety of plants such as broccoli, cauliflower, and cabbage. Caulanthus flavescens is further classified as a member of the genus Caulanthus, which consists of several species of annual and perennial plants. The genus Caulanthus is a part of the tribe Cardamineae, which includes plants with similar characteristics such as four-petaled flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Caulanthus flavescens is native to the western United States and is often found growing in open areas and along roadsides.

intro: Caulanthus flavescens, also known as yellow flixweed, is a perennial plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in reducing swelling and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Caulanthus flavescens include isothiocyanates, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Caulanthus flavescens is often prepared as a tincture, infused oil, or dried herb, and is used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
